You said:

So, I am creating a form using React formik and Yup validation and Accordion. I am using these four things to create one simple form that has four steps and when I fill the first step, there should be a button like in the form, there are four steps, there are like four different forms. First step, second step, third step, and fourth step. And when we open the page for the form, we go on the first step, then we have a few details, XYZ details. Then after submitting, after filling that details, we do have at bottom our button in which like we have our clear button and next button. And if we click on next button, then it should go to the next form or you can say step 2. And step 2 has its own details and after filling that details, we do have the same clear button and next button. And if we click next button, it should go to the step 3 and it should go to the step 4. And I am using Tailwind CSS to use this and give me a proper folder structure to implement these things. And I am currently, like I am thinking to use, as I said, that I am using React, JS and Formic and Yup validation and Tailwind CSS. I hope you have understood the assignment.

ChatGPT said:

Yeah, I get exactly what you’re building — a multi-step form (wizard) using React + Formik + Yup + Tailwind + Accordion UI. Good choice of stack 👍

Let’s make this clean, scalable, and not messy later.

🧠 Core Idea (How to Structure This)

Instead of making 4 separate forms, treat it as:

  • One Formik form

  • Split into 4 steps (components)

  • Controlled by a step state

  • Validation changes per step

📁 Recommended Folder Structure

Keep things modular and readable:

src/
│
├── components/
│   ├── form/
│   │   ├── MultiStepForm.jsx
│   │   ├── Stepper.jsx (optional progress UI)
│   │   ├── FormButtons.jsx
│   │
│   │   ├── steps/
│   │   │   ├── StepOne.jsx
│   │   │   ├── StepTwo.jsx
│   │   │   ├── StepThree.jsx
│   │   │   └── StepFour.jsx
│
├── validations/
│   ├── stepOneSchema.js
│   ├── stepTwoSchema.js
│   ├── stepThreeSchema.js
│   └── stepFourSchema.js
│
├── constants/
│   └── initialValues.js
│
├── pages/
│   └── FormPage.jsx
│
├── hooks/
│   └── useMultiStepForm.js (optional but clean)
│
└── App.jsx

⚙️ Step Flow Logic

You’ll maintain:

JavaScript
const [step, setStep] = useState(0);

🧩 MultiStepForm.jsx (Main Controller)

This is the brain.

JavaScript
import { Formik, Form } from "formik";
import { useState } from "react";

import StepOne from "./steps/StepOne";
import StepTwo from "./steps/StepTwo";
import StepThree from "./steps/StepThree";
import StepFour from "./steps/StepFour";

import { stepOneSchema } from "../../validations/stepOneSchema";
import { stepTwoSchema } from "../../validations/stepTwoSchema";
import { stepThreeSchema } from "../../validations/stepThreeSchema";
import { stepFourSchema } from "../../validations/stepFourSchema";

import initialValues from "../../constants/initialValues";
import FormButtons from "./FormButtons";

const steps = [
  StepOne,
  StepTwo,
  StepThree,
  StepFour
];

const validationSchemas = [
  stepOneSchema,
  stepTwoSchema,
  stepThreeSchema,
  stepFourSchema
];

export default function MultiStepForm() {
  const [step, setStep] = useState(0);
  const CurrentStep = steps[step];

  const isLastStep = step === steps.length - 1;

  const handleNext = (values) => {
    if (!isLastStep) {
      setStep((prev) => prev + 1);
    } else {
      console.log("Final Submit:", values);
    }
  };

  const handleBack = () => {
    setStep((prev) => prev - 1);
  };

  return (
    <Formik
      initialValues={initialValues}
      validationSchema={validationSchemas[step]}
      onSubmit={handleNext}
    >
      {(formik) => (
        <Form className="p-6 bg-white rounded-xl shadow-md space-y-6">
          <CurrentStep />

          <FormButtons
            step={step}
            isLastStep={isLastStep}
            handleBack={handleBack}
            formik={formik}
          />
        </Form>
      )}
    </Formik>
  );
}

🔘 FormButtons.jsx

JavaScript
export default function FormButtons({
  step,
  isLastStep,
  handleBack,
  formik
}) {
  return (
    <div className="flex justify-between mt-4">
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => formik.resetForm()}
        className="px-4 py-2 bg-gray-300 rounded"
      >
        Clear
      </button>

      <div className="space-x-2">
        {step > 0 && (
          <button
            type="button"
            onClick={handleBack}
            className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-300 rounded"
          >
            Back
          </button>
        )}

        <button
          type="submit"
          className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-500 text-white rounded"
        >
          {isLastStep ? "Submit" : "Next"}
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

🧾 Example Step Component (StepOne.jsx)

JavaScript
import { Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik";

export default function StepOne() {
  return (
    <div>
      <h2 className="text-xl font-bold mb-4">Step 1</h2>

      <div>
        <label>Name</label>
        <Field
          name="name"
          className="border p-2 w-full"
        />
        <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

✅ Yup Validation Example

JavaScript
import * as Yup from "yup";

export const stepOneSchema = Yup.object({
  name: Yup.string().required("Name is required")
});

🧱 initialValues.js

JavaScript
export default {
  name: "",
  email: "",
  age: "",
  address: ""
};

🎯 Important Design Decisions

✅ Why single Formik instead of 4 forms?

  • Keeps all data in one place

  • Easy final submission

  • No state syncing issues

✅ Why step-based validation?

JavaScript
validationSchema={validationSchemas[step]}

  • Only validates current step

  • Prevents unnecessary errors

✅ Accordion Integration

Each step can be wrapped like:

JavaScript
<div className={`${active ? "block" : "hidden"}`}>
  <StepOne />
</div>

OR use Tailwind collapse styles.

🚀 Bonus Improvements

If you want this to feel like a real production app, you can add:

  • Progress bar (Stepper.jsx)

  • Save progress (localStorage)

  • Animations (Framer Motion)

  • Disable next button until valid:

JavaScript
disabled={!formik.isValid}

🧩 Mental Model

Think of it like:

Formik (global form state)
   ↓
Step Controller (step state)
   ↓
Step Components (UI)
   ↓
Validation per step

If you want, I can next:

✅ Add Accordion UI with Tailwind
✅ Add progress bar (like stepper at top)
✅ Show full working example (copy-paste ready)
✅ Or debug your current code

Just tell me 👍


You said:

See, don't start doing it. you have already wrote it, by the way, but let's go step by step and they are not like steps. Like this is a banking form in which we have four steps. The first step is bank and letter of guarantee details, and the second one is applicant and delivery details, and the third one is beneficiary and liability details, and the fourth one is total order amount details. So we have these four things, and I want us to go step by step and change the folder structure a little bit so that it should look like a proper thing. Like that previously what I told was a layman, and even this is a layman, but I hope you have understood.

ChatGPT said:

Got it — this is not just a generic multi-step form anymore, this is a domain-based banking form. That changes how we should structure things 👍

Right now your earlier structure was step-based (StepOne, StepTwo…), which is fine for demos, but not good for real-world apps.

We’ll fix that by making it:

👉 Feature-driven (domain-based) instead of step-based

🧠 First Principle (Important)

Instead of naming things like:

❌ StepOne.jsx
❌ StepTwo.jsx

We use:

✅ BankGuaranteeDetails.jsx
✅ ApplicantDetails.jsx
✅ BeneficiaryDetails.jsx
✅ OrderAmountDetails.jsx

This makes your code:

  • readable

  • scalable

  • professional (important for interviews/jobs)

📁 Updated Folder Structure (Clean + Real-World)

src/
│
├── features/
│   └── bankingForm/
│       │
│       ├── components/
│       │   ├── BankingForm.jsx          // main controller
│       │   ├── FormNavigation.jsx       // next/back/clear buttons
│       │   ├── FormStepper.jsx          // optional UI (accordion/steps)
│       │
│       ├── sections/                   // 🔥 domain-based sections
│       │   ├── BankGuaranteeDetails.jsx
│       │   ├── ApplicantDeliveryDetails.jsx
│       │   ├── BeneficiaryLiabilityDetails.jsx
│       │   └── TotalOrderAmountDetails.jsx
│       │
│       ├── validation/
│       │   ├── bankGuarantee.schema.js
│       │   ├── applicant.schema.js
│       │   ├── beneficiary.schema.js
│       │   └── orderAmount.schema.js
│       │
│       ├── constants/
│       │   ├── initialValues.js
│       │   └── formSteps.js            // step config
│       │
│       └── hooks/
│           └── useBankingForm.js       // step logic (optional)
│
├── pages/
│   └── BankingFormPage.jsx
│
├── App.jsx

🧩 Why This Structure is Better

✅ 1. “sections” instead of “steps”

Because:

  • Business logic ≠ UI steps

  • Tomorrow order may change, but sections remain same

✅ 2. Feature-based folder (bankingForm)

This is how real companies structure React apps

Instead of:

components/
validations/

We group everything related to this form in one place.

✅ 3. Validation tied to domain

bankGuarantee.schema.js

Not:

step1Schema.js

Because step ≠ meaning.

✅ 4. Config-driven steps (VERY IMPORTANT)

We don’t hardcode steps anymore.

JavaScript
import BankGuaranteeDetails from "../sections/BankGuaranteeDetails";
import ApplicantDeliveryDetails from "../sections/ApplicantDeliveryDetails";
import BeneficiaryLiabilityDetails from "../sections/BeneficiaryLiabilityDetails";
import TotalOrderAmountDetails from "../sections/TotalOrderAmountDetails";

import { bankGuaranteeSchema } from "../validation/bankGuarantee.schema";
import { applicantSchema } from "../validation/applicant.schema";
import { beneficiarySchema } from "../validation/beneficiary.schema";
import { orderAmountSchema } from "../validation/orderAmount.schema";

export const FORM_STEPS = [
  {
    id: "bankDetails",
    label: "Bank & Guarantee",
    component: BankGuaranteeDetails,
    validationSchema: bankGuaranteeSchema,
  },
  {
    id: "applicant",
    label: "Applicant & Delivery",
    component: ApplicantDeliveryDetails,
    validationSchema: applicantSchema,
  },
  {
    id: "beneficiary",
    label: "Beneficiary & Liability",
    component: BeneficiaryLiabilityDetails,
    validationSchema: beneficiarySchema,
  },
  {
    id: "order",
    label: "Total Order Amount",
    component: TotalOrderAmountDetails,
    validationSchema: orderAmountSchema,
  },
];

🧠 Why This is Powerful

Now your form becomes:

👉 dynamic
👉 scalable
👉 clean

Instead of:

if (step === 1) ...

You do:

JavaScript
const currentStep = FORM_STEPS[step];

🧩 What We’ll Do Next (Step-by-Step Plan)

Since you want to go gradually, here’s how we proceed:

👉 Step 1 (next discussion)

We design:

  • initialValues.js

  • based on your banking fields

👉 Step 2

Build:

  • FORM_STEPS config

  • connect Formik with dynamic validation

👉 Step 3

Build:

  • section components (clean + reusable inputs)

👉 Step 4

Add:

  • Accordion UI (Tailwind)

  • Stepper UI

👉 Step 5

Enhancements:

  • disable next

  • save progress

  • API submit

👍 Before Moving Ahead

I need one thing from you:

👉 Tell me the fields inside Step 1 (Bank & Guarantee Details)

Example:

  • bank name?

  • guarantee number?

  • issue date?

  • expiry date?

Once you give that, we’ll:

👉 design perfect initialValues + Yup schema (real-world level)

Then we’ll move step by step exactly like you want.


You said:

see before moving i am do not know about formik and yup validation i do know react good. also is this schema Structure a part of yup validations ?

ChatGPT said:

Good, this is actually the right place to pause and clarify — because if you don’t understand Formik + Yup basics, the structure I gave will feel confusing.

Let’s simplify this properly.

🧠 First: What is Formik?

Think of Formik as:

👉 A form manager for React

Instead of you doing this manually:

JavaScript
const [name, setName] = useState("");
const [email, setEmail] = useState("");

Formik does:

  • stores all form values

  • handles change

  • handles submit

  • handles validation

🧠 What is Yup?

👉 Yup is only for validation

It checks things like:

  • required fields

  • email format

  • number limits

❓ Your Question:

is this schema structure a part of yup validations?

👉 YES — exactly.

When you saw:

JavaScript
bankGuarantee.schema.js

That file contains a Yup schema.

🧩 What is a “Schema”?

Schema = rules for your form fields

Example:

JavaScript
import * as Yup from "yup";

export const bankGuaranteeSchema = Yup.object({
  bankName: Yup.string().required("Bank name is required"),
  guaranteeNumber: Yup.string().required("Guarantee number is required"),
});

👉 This means:

  • bankName must be filled

  • guaranteeNumber must be filled

🧠 How Formik + Yup Work Together

Formik uses Yup like this:

JavaScript
<Formik
  initialValues={{ bankName: "", guaranteeNumber: "" }}
  validationSchema={bankGuaranteeSchema}
  onSubmit={handleSubmit}
>

👉 Flow:

  1. User types input

  2. Formik stores values

  3. Yup checks rules (schema)

  4. Errors are shown automatically

🔁 Simple Mental Model

Formik = form state + handling  
Yup = validation rules  
Schema = object where rules are written

🧩 Why We Created Separate Schema Files

Instead of doing everything in one file:

❌ Bad:

JavaScript
validationSchema = Yup.object({ ...100 fields... })

We split:

bankGuarantee.schema.js
applicant.schema.js
beneficiary.schema.js

👉 Because your form has different sections (banking domain)

⚠️ Important Reality Check

Right now you said:

I don’t know Formik and Yup

So if we jump directly into:

  • multi-step

  • accordion

  • dynamic schema

👉 it will confuse you later.

✅ Better Approach (Recommended)

Before continuing structure, we should do:

Step A (very small)

Build:
👉 one simple Formik form
👉 with 2–3 fields
👉 with Yup validation

Step B

Then:
👉 convert it into multi-step

Step C

Then:
👉 apply the folder structure we designed

👍 What I Suggest Next

Let’s not rush into architecture yet.

👉 I’ll teach you in 10 minutes practical way:

  • basic Formik form

  • basic Yup validation

  • error handling

Then everything else will feel EASY.

👉 Tell me this:

Do you want:

Option 1:

👉 Quick crash course (best)
→ simple form (name + email + validation)

Option 2:

👉 Continue directly with your banking form
(but slightly harder to follow)

I recommend Option 1, but I’ll follow your pace 👍


You said:

lets go with option 1

ChatGPT said:

Perfect — this is the smartest move 👍
We’ll keep this very simple and practical, no over-engineering.

🎯 Goal (What you’ll learn in 10 mins)

We’ll build:

👉 A small form with:

  • Name

  • Email

  • Submit button

  • Validation (required + email format)

🧱 Step 1: Install Packages

Bash
npm install formik yup

🧩 Step 2: Basic Formik Form

Create a file:

Bash
SimpleForm.jsx

✨ Full Working Code (Read slowly)

JavaScript
import { Formik, Form, Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik";
import * as Yup from "yup";

export default function SimpleForm() {
  return (
    <Formik
      initialValues={{
        name: "",
        email: "",
      }}

      validationSchema={Yup.object({
        name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"),
        email: Yup.string()
          .email("Invalid email")
          .required("Email is required"),
      })}

      onSubmit={(values) => {
        console.log("Form Data:", values);
      }}
    >
      <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4">

        {/* Name */}
        <div>
          <label>Name</label>
          <Field
            name="name"
            className="border p-2 w-full"
          />
          <ErrorMessage
            name="name"
            component="div"
            className="text-red-500"
          />
        </div>

        {/* Email */}
        <div>
          <label>Email</label>
          <Field
            name="email"
            type="email"
            className="border p-2 w-full"
          />
          <ErrorMessage
            name="email"
            component="div"
            className="text-red-500"
          />
        </div>

        <button
          type="submit"
          className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded"
        >
          Submit
        </button>

      </Form>
    </Formik>
  );
}

🧠 Now Understand This (Very Important)

1️⃣ initialValues

JavaScript
initialValues={{
  name: "",
  email: ""
}}

👉 This replaces useState
Formik stores everything here.

2️⃣ validationSchema

JavaScript
Yup.object({
  name: Yup.string().required(),
  email: Yup.string().email().required()
})

👉 This is your rules (schema)

3️⃣ <Field />

JavaScript
<Field name="name" />

👉 Automatically:

  • connects input to Formik

  • updates values

  • no need for onChange

4️⃣ <ErrorMessage />

JavaScript
<ErrorMessage name="name" />

👉 Shows validation error automatically

5️⃣ <Form />

Replaces:

JavaScript
<form onSubmit={...}>

Formik handles submit internally.

6️⃣ onSubmit

JavaScript
onSubmit={(values) => {
  console.log(values);
}}

👉 You get all form data here

🔁 What Happens When User Types?

User types → Formik stores value  
Click submit → Yup validates  
If error → shown via ErrorMessage  
If success → onSubmit runs

🧪 Try This Yourself

After writing this:

👉 Leave fields empty → click submit
👉 Enter wrong email → see error
👉 Enter correct → check console

⚠️ Common Beginner Mistakes

❌ Using useState with Formik
❌ Forgetting name attribute
❌ Not matching initialValues with fields
❌ Writing validation outside Yup

🚀 Next Step (Don’t Skip)

Once you're comfortable:

👉 Next we will:

Convert THIS into 2-step form

  • Step 1 → Name

  • Step 2 → Email

  • Next / Back buttons

That will directly prepare you for your banking form

👍 Your Turn

Before we move ahead, tell me:

👉 Did you understand:

  • what Formik does

  • what Yup does

  • what schema is

If yes → we go to multi-step version (very important)
If not → I’ll simplify further without rushing 👍


You said:

understood most of it took more then 10 mins my bad 🥲 brfore you started creating i'll show yow expexted images 1. when form is filled and we click on next 2. when the form is opened 3. when form is closed

ChatGPT said:

No worries at all — this stuff always takes more than 10 mins the first time 😄
And these screenshots actually help a LOT. Now I clearly see what you're building.

🧠 What You’re Actually Building (Now Clear)

This is not just a form — it’s:

👉 Accordion + Multi-step Form (combined)

Each section has 3 states:

🎯 1. Completed State (after clicking Next)

(Your first image)

Behavior:

  • Section is collapsed

  • Shows ✅ check icon

  • Shows Edit button

  • Data is already saved

🎯 2. Active/Open State (current step)

(Your second image)

Behavior:

  • Section is expanded

  • User fills inputs

  • Shows:

    • Clear button

    • Next button

🎯 3. Closed/Upcoming State

(Your third image)

Behavior:

  • Section is collapsed

  • Looks disabled / inactive

  • User cannot interact yet

🧠 Key Insight (VERY IMPORTANT)

This is NOT just:

step = 1, 2, 3

Instead, each section has a status:

JavaScript
status = "completed" | "active" | "inactive"

🧩 So Your Logic Becomes

For 4 sections:

JavaScript
const currentStep = 1;

Then:

SectionStatus
0completed
1active
2inactive
3inactive

🧠 Real Mental Model

Formik → stores all form data

Step State → controls UI (accordion behavior)

Sections → render based on status

⚠️ Important Shift (This is where beginners get stuck)

Earlier we were thinking:

❌ “Go to next page”

Now it is:

✅ “Collapse current section → mark complete → open next section”

🧩 What We Will Build Next (Step-by-Step, no rush)

Now that you understand Formik basics, next we do:

👉 Step 1 (next message)

We will build:

✅ ONLY 2 sections (not 4 yet)
✅ With:

  • open / close behavior

  • next button

  • completed state

👉 No Tailwind styling focus yet
👉 Only logic

👉 Step 2

Add:

  • Yup validation per section

👉 Step 3

Convert into:

  • your full 4-section banking form

👉 Step 4

Polish UI:

  • Tailwind

  • icons

  • edit button

👍 Before Moving Ahead

Tell me:

👉 Do you understand this concept:

  • completed vs active vs inactive

  • accordion instead of page navigation

If yes → I’ll build Step 1 (2-section working version)
If not → I’ll simplify it even more (no problem 👍)


You said:

before that can you explain me the assignment properly such that if i have to explain you again you can understand easily this a temp chat i fear it may go

ChatGPT said:

That’s actually a very smart thing to do 👍 — if you can explain it clearly, you fully understand it.

Let me convert your whole assignment into a clean, professional explanation that you can reuse anytime (even in interviews).

🧠 Your Assignment (Clear Explanation)

You are building a:

👉 Multi-section banking form using React, Formik, Yup, and Tailwind CSS

🎯 Core Requirement

The form is divided into 4 sections (not pages):

  1. Bank & Letter of Guarantee Details

  2. Applicant & Delivery Details

  3. Beneficiary & Liability Details

  4. Total Order Amount Details

🔁 How the Form Works (Flow)

Instead of navigating between pages:

👉 The form behaves like an accordion stepper

🧩 Section States

Each section can be in one of 3 states:

1. 🟢 Active (Open)

  • Current section user is filling

  • Inputs are visible

  • Shows:

    • Clear button

    • Next button

2. ✅ Completed (Collapsed)

  • User clicked Next

  • Section closes

  • Shows:

    • ✔️ Check icon

    • Edit button

  • Data is saved

3. ⚪ Inactive (Locked)

  • Future sections

  • Collapsed

  • Not clickable

🔄 User Flow Example

  1. Page loads
    → Section 1 = Active
    → Section 2,3,4 = Inactive

  2. User fills Section 1 → clicks Next
    → Section 1 = Completed
    → Section 2 = Active

  3. Repeat until Section 4

🧠 Technical Requirements

🔹 React

  • Build UI components for each section

🔹 Formik

  • Manage:

    • form state

    • input values

    • submission

🔹 Yup

  • Validate each section separately

🔹 Tailwind CSS

  • Style:

    • accordion

    • buttons

    • inputs

🧩 Key Implementation Concept

👉 Single Formik form
👉 Multiple sections inside it

NOT:

4 different forms

BUT:

1 form → split into 4 sections

🧠 State You Will Maintain

1. Current Step

JavaScript
const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0);

2. Section Status Logic

JavaScript
if (index < currentStep) → completed  
if (index === currentStep) → active  
if (index > currentStep) → inactive

🧩 Folder Structure (Concept Level)

bankingForm/
  sections/
    BankGuaranteeDetails
    ApplicantDeliveryDetails
    BeneficiaryLiabilityDetails
    TotalOrderAmountDetails

  validation/
    schemas for each section

  components/
    main form + navigation

🧠 One-Line Summary (Very Important)

👉
“It is a single Formik form divided into multiple accordion sections, where each section becomes completed, active, or inactive based on user progress.”

🎤 If You Had to Explain This to Me Again

You can just say:

"I’m building a multi-step accordion form where each section represents part of a banking workflow. Only one section is active at a time, previous ones collapse as completed, and future ones stay disabled. I’m using Formik for state management and Yup for validation."

👍 You’re Ready

Now you:

  • understand the problem ✔️

  • understand the flow ✔️

  • understand the tools ✔️

👉 Next step (when you say go):

We’ll build 2-section working version (super important foundation)
No overwhelm, just clean logic.


You said:

thanks very well written now i am having a doubt should we implement accordion now only ?? or paralelly with formik and Yup ? btw i like your the idea of 4 steps

ChatGPT said:

Great question — this is exactly the kind of decision that makes or breaks your implementation 👍

🧠 Short Answer

👉 Do NOT implement full accordion UI yet

Instead:

✅ Build Formik + step logic first
➕ Add accordion behavior alongside it (basic, not styled)
❌ Don’t focus on UI/design yet

🧩 Why This Order is Important

If you start with accordion first:

❌ You’ll struggle with:

  • state syncing

  • validation per section

  • next/back logic

  • data persistence

If you start with Formik + steps:

✅ Everything becomes predictable:

  • form state is centralized

  • validation is controlled

  • navigation is clean

🧠 Best Approach (Real Dev Workflow)

✅ Phase 1: Core Logic (NOW)

Build:

  • Formik form

  • currentStep state

  • Next button

  • Show/hide sections using simple conditions

👉 Example:

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && <Section1 />}
{currentStep === 1 && <Section2 />}

No accordion yet. Just logic.

✅ Phase 2: Add Accordion Behavior

Now convert:

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && <Section1 />}

Into:

JavaScript
<div>
  {/* Header */}
  <div onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}>
    Section 1
  </div>

  {/* Body */}
  {currentStep === 0 && <Section1 />}
</div>

👉 This becomes your accordion

✅ Phase 3: Add Status (🔥 Important)

Now introduce:

JavaScript
const getStatus = (index) => {
  if (index < currentStep) return "completed";
  if (index === currentStep) return "active";
  return "inactive";
};

✅ Phase 4: Tailwind Styling

Now finally:

  • colors

  • icons

  • spacing

  • polish UI

🎯 Key Insight

👉 Accordion is just UI

👉 Your real problem is:

  • step control

  • validation

  • state management

🧠 Your Current Plan (Best)

We follow this:

  1. ✅ Learn Formik (done)

  2. 👉 Build 2-step form (next)

  3. 👉 Add step logic (currentStep)

  4. 👉 Convert into accordion

  5. 👉 Scale to 4 sections

  6. 👉 Add styling

👍 Final Recommendation

👉 Build like this:

Logic first → Structure → Behavior → UI

NOT:

UI → Logic → Fix bugs forever 😅

🚀 Next Step

If you're ready:

👉 I’ll help you build 2-section working version (Formik + Next button)

This is the foundation of everything.

Just say: "let's build" and we go step-by-step (no overwhelm).


You said:

yes let's build

ChatGPT said:

Perfect — now we build 💪
No rush, no complexity. Just clean foundation.

🎯 What We’re Building (Step 1)

👉 A 2-section form:

  • Section 1 → Name

  • Section 2 → Email

👉 With:

  • Next button

  • Back button

  • Shared Formik state

👉 No accordion UI yet (just logic)

🧱 Step 1: Create File

Bash
MultiStepForm.jsx

🧩 Step 2: Full Working Code

👉 Copy this first, then we’ll break it down:

JavaScript
import { Formik, Form, Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik";
import { useState } from "react";
import * as Yup from "yup";

export default function MultiStepForm() {
  const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0);

  const isLastStep = currentStep === 1;

  const validationSchemas = [
    Yup.object({
      name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"),
    }),
    Yup.object({
      email: Yup.string()
        .email("Invalid email")
        .required("Email is required"),
    }),
  ];

  return (
    <Formik
      initialValues={{
        name: "",
        email: "",
      }}
      validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]}
      onSubmit={(values) => {
        if (!isLastStep) {
          setCurrentStep((prev) => prev + 1);
        } else {
          console.log("Final Data:", values);
        }
      }}
    >
      {(formik) => (
        <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4">

          {/* STEP 1 */}
          {currentStep === 0 && (
            <div>
              <h2 className="text-xl font-bold">Step 1</h2>

              <Field
                name="name"
                placeholder="Enter name"
                className="border p-2 w-full"
              />
              <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
            </div>
          )}

          {/* STEP 2 */}
          {currentStep === 1 && (
            <div>
              <h2 className="text-xl font-bold">Step 2</h2>

              <Field
                name="email"
                placeholder="Enter email"
                className="border p-2 w-full"
              />
              <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
            </div>
          )}

          {/* BUTTONS */}
          <div className="flex justify-between">

            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={() => formik.resetForm()}
              className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
            >
              Clear
            </button>

            <div className="space-x-2">
              {currentStep > 0 && (
                <button
                  type="button"
                  onClick={() => setCurrentStep((prev) => prev - 1)}
                  className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
                >
                  Back
                </button>
              )}

              <button
                type="submit"
                className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded"
              >
                {isLastStep ? "Submit" : "Next"}
              </button>
            </div>
          </div>

        </Form>
      )}
    </Formik>
  );
}

🧠 Now Understand What You Just Built

✅ 1. Step Control

JavaScript
const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0);

👉 Controls which section is visible

✅ 2. Dynamic Validation (🔥 Important)

JavaScript
validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]}

👉 Step 1 → validates name
👉 Step 2 → validates email

✅ 3. Step Switching

JavaScript
if (!isLastStep) {
  setCurrentStep(prev => prev + 1);
}

👉 This is your Next button logic

✅ 4. Shared Form State

Even when you move to Step 2:

👉 Name is still saved
👉 No data loss

✅ 5. Conditional Rendering

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && <Step1 />}
{currentStep === 1 && <Step2 />}

👉 This will later become your accordion sections

🧪 Test This Properly

Try this:

  1. Click Next without entering name → ❌ error

  2. Enter name → Next → go to Step 2

  3. Go back → name still there ✅

  4. Enter email → Submit → check console

⚠️ Important Learning Moment

👉 You just built the core engine of your banking form

Everything else (accordion, UI, icons) is just decoration on top of this.

🚀 Next Step (Very Important)

Now we will:

👉 Convert this into accordion-style behavior

  • show both sections

  • only one open

  • previous becomes “completed”

👍 Before Moving Ahead

Tell me:

👉 Did this work on your side?

If yes → next we’ll turn this into your real UI behavior (accordion + completed state)
If something broke → send error, we fix it together 👨‍💻


You said:

i could understand 80% of it now and yes its working

ChatGPT said:

That’s actually perfect — 80% understanding at this stage is exactly where you should be 👍
The remaining 20% will click once we evolve this into accordion behavior.

🎯 What We Do Now (Big Step 🚀)

We will convert your current form into:

👉 Accordion-style multi-section form

BUT still with only 2 sections (to keep it simple)

🧠 What Will Change?

Right now you have:

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && <Step1 />}
{currentStep === 1 && <Step2 />}

👉 Only ONE section exists in DOM

Now we’ll make:

👉 BOTH sections always visible
👉 But:

  • one is open (active)

  • one is closed (completed/inactive)

🧩 New Concept: Status Function

Add this at top:

JavaScript
const getStatus = (index) => {
  if (index < currentStep) return "completed";
  if (index === currentStep) return "active";
  return "inactive";
};

🧱 Updated UI Structure (Important)

Replace your step rendering with this:

🧩 Section 1

JavaScript
const status1 = getStatus(0);

<div className="border p-4 rounded">
  <div className="flex justify-between items-center">
    <h2>Step 1</h2>

    {status1 === "completed" && (
      <button onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}>Edit</button>
    )}
  </div>

  {status1 === "active" && (
    <div>
      <Field name="name" className="border p-2 w-full" />
      <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
    </div>
  )}
</div>

🧩 Section 2

JavaScript
const status2 = getStatus(1);

<div className="border p-4 rounded mt-4">
  <h2>Step 2</h2>

  {status2 === "active" && (
    <div>
      <Field name="email" className="border p-2 w-full" />
      <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
    </div>
  )}
</div>

🔘 Buttons (Keep Same)

Your buttons logic stays SAME — just move it inside active section if you want later.

🧠 What You Just Achieved

Now your UI behaves like:

StepState
0active
1inactive

Then after clicking Next:

StepState
0✅ completed
1🔓 active

🔥 Key Behavior You Just Built

👉 Previous step collapses
👉 Next step opens
👉 Edit button lets you go back

⚠️ Don’t Overthink Styling Yet

Right now:

  • no icons needed

  • no fancy Tailwind

  • just logic

🧪 Test This Carefully

Do this:

  1. Fill Step 1 → Next

  2. Step 1 should collapse

  3. Step 2 opens

  4. Click Edit → Step 1 opens again

🧠 Small Reality Check

If something feels confusing:

👉 It’s NOT Formik
👉 It’s just UI state (currentStep + status)

🚀 Next Step (After This Works)

We’ll:

👉 Move buttons inside each section (like your real UI)
👉 Add:

  • Clear button per section

  • Next button per section

Then:

👉 Scale to your real 4 banking sections

👍 Your Turn

Try implementing this.

If:

  • it works → we move forward

  • something breaks → paste your code, I’ll fix it with you

You're doing this the right way 👏

🧠 What Confused You

This part probably felt confusing:

JavaScript
getStatus()

and

JavaScript
status === "active"

👉 So forget that for now. We don’t need it yet.

🎯 Let’s Rebuild Slowly (Super Simple)

We already have:

👉 working 2-step form
👉 with currentStep

Now we just make a very small change:

🧩 Step 1: Show BOTH sections always

Instead of:

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && <Step1 />}
{currentStep === 1 && <Step2 />}

👉 We write both:

✅ Replace With This

JavaScript
{/* SECTION 1 */}
<div className="border p-4">
  <h2>Step 1</h2>

  {currentStep === 0 && (
    <>
      <Field name="name" className="border p-2 w-full" />
      <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
    </>
  )}
</div>

{/* SECTION 2 */}
<div className="border p-4 mt-4">
  <h2>Step 2</h2>

  {currentStep === 1 && (
    <>
      <Field name="email" className="border p-2 w-full" />
      <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
    </>
  )}
</div>

🧠 What This Does

StepWhat You See
0Step 1 open, Step 2 closed
1Step 1 closed, Step 2 open

👉 That’s already accordion behavior (basic)

🎯 That’s It. Nothing More.

No:

  • status

  • edit button

  • complexity

  2. Click Next

  3. Step 1 hides, Step 2 opens

You said:

ChatGPT said:

Good — this is actually the best place to debug, not later 👍
Let’s fix this together step by step. Don’t worry.

🔍 First — Most Common Issue

When you replaced your code, you probably:

❌ Removed something important
OR
❌ Put JSX in wrong place

✅ Let’s Reset to Working Version (Safe Fix)

👉 Copy this complete version (don’t mix with old code)

🧩 Clean Working Code (Accordion Basic)

JavaScript
import { Formik, Form, Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik";
import { useState } from "react";
import * as Yup from "yup";

export default function MultiStepForm() {
  const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0);

  const isLastStep = currentStep === 1;

  const validationSchemas = [
    Yup.object({
      name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"),
    }),
    Yup.object({
      email: Yup.string()
        .email("Invalid email")
        .required("Email is required"),
    }),
  ];

  return (
    <Formik
      initialValues={{ name: "", email: "" }}
      validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]}
      onSubmit={(values) => {
        if (!isLastStep) {
          setCurrentStep((prev) => prev + 1);
        } else {
          console.log("Final Data:", values);
        }
      }}
    >
      {(formik) => (
        <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4">

          {/* SECTION 1 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <h2 className="font-bold">Step 1</h2>

            {currentStep === 0 && (
              <>
                <Field
                  name="name"
                  placeholder="Enter name"
                  className="border p-2 w-full"
                />
                <ErrorMessage
                  name="name"
                  component="div"
                  className="text-red-500"
                />
              </>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* SECTION 2 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <h2 className="font-bold">Step 2</h2>

            {currentStep === 1 && (
              <>
                <Field
                  name="email"
                  placeholder="Enter email"
                  className="border p-2 w-full"
                />
                <ErrorMessage
                  name="email"
                  component="div"
                  className="text-red-500"
                />
              </>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* BUTTONS */}
          <div className="flex justify-between">
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={() => formik.resetForm()}
              className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
            >
              Clear
            </button>

            <div className="space-x-2">
              {currentStep > 0 && (
                <button
                  type="button"
                  onClick={() => setCurrentStep((prev) => prev - 1)}
                  className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
                >
                  Back
                </button>
              )}

              <button
                type="submit"
                className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded"
              >
                {isLastStep ? "Submit" : "Next"}
              </button>
            </div>
          </div>

        </Form>
      )}
    </Formik>
  );
}

🧠 If It STILL Doesn’t Run

Check these quickly:

✅ 1. Installed packages?

Bash
npm install formik yup

✅ 2. Any console error?

👉 Copy error and send here
I’ll fix instantly

✅ 3. Common mistakes

  • ❌ Forgot import { useState }

  • ❌ Missed return ( ... )

  • ❌ JSX not wrapped properly

  • ❌ Deleted <Form> accidentally

🧪 What You Should See

  • Step 1 open

  • Step 2 closed

  • Click Next → Step 2 opens

👍 Don’t Worry

This part is:
👉 the hardest transition (Formik → multi-step UI)

Once this works:
👉 everything becomes easy from here

👉 Your Move

If it works → say “working now”
If error → paste error or screenshot

I’ll debug it with you step-by-step 👨‍💻

You said:

import { Formik, Form, Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik"; import { useState } from "react"; import * as Yup from "yup"; export default function MultiStepForm() { const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0); const isLastStep = currentStep === 1; const validationSchemas = [ Yup.object({ name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"), age: Yup.number().required("age is required") }), Yup.object({ email: Yup.string() .email("Invalid email") .required("Email is required"), }), ]; return ( <Formik initialValues={{ name: "", email: "" }} validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]} onSubmit={(values) => { if (!isLastStep) { setCurrentStep((prev) => prev + 1); } else { console.log("Final Data:", values); } }} > {(formik) => ( <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4"> {/* SECTION 1 */} <div className="border p-4"> <h2 className="font-bold">Step 1</h2> {currentStep === 0 && ( <> <Field name="name" placeholder="Enter name" className="border p-2 w-full" /> <Field name="age" placeholder="age" className="border p-2 w-full" /> <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> </> )} </div> {/* SECTION 2 */} <div className="border p-4"> <h2 className="font-bold">Step 2</h2> {currentStep === 1 && ( <> <Field name="email" placeholder="Enter email" className="border p-2 w-full" /> <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> </> )} </div> {/* BUTTONS */} <div className="flex justify-between"> <button type="button" onClick={() => formik.resetForm()} className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Clear </button> <div className="space-x-2"> {currentStep > 0 && ( <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep((prev) => prev - 1)} className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Back </button> )} <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded" > {isLastStep ? "Submit" : "Next"} </button> </div> </div> </Form> )} </Formik> ); } i think i did correct

ChatGPT said:

You did really well — this is exactly the kind of practice I wanted you to do 👏
And yes, you’re 90% correct.

Let’s refine it like a developer (small but important fixes).

✅ What You Did Right

  • ✔️ Added age field

  • ✔️ Updated Yup validation

  • ✔️ Added <Field />

  • ✔️ Form still works → BIG win

👉 This means you understand:

  • Formik flow

  • Yup basics

  • Step logic

⚠️ Small Issues (Important Fixes)

❌ 1. Missing age in initialValues

Right now:

JavaScript
initialValues={{ name: "", email: "" }}

👉 But you added age, so Formik doesn’t know about it initially.

✅ Fix:

JavaScript
initialValues={{ name: "", age: "", email: "" }}

❌ 2. Age validation type issue

JavaScript
age: Yup.number().required("age is required")

👉 Problem:

  • Empty input = "" → not a number

  • Yup may throw weird errors

✅ Better Version (IMPORTANT)

JavaScript
age: Yup.number()
  .typeError("Age must be a number")
  .required("Age is required")

❌ 3. Missing type="number" in input

Right now:

JavaScript
<Field name="age" />

👉 This is treated like text

✅ Fix:

JavaScript
<Field
  name="age"
  type="number"
  placeholder="Age"
  className="border p-2 w-full"
/>

⚠️ 4. ErrorMessage Order (Minor UX)

You wrote:

JavaScript
<ErrorMessage name="age" />
<ErrorMessage name="name" />

👉 Better UX:

JavaScript
<Field name="name" />
<ErrorMessage name="name" />

<Field name="age" />
<ErrorMessage name="age" />

🧠 Final Polished Version (Step 1 part)

JavaScript
<Field
  name="name"
  placeholder="Enter name"
  className="border p-2 w-full"
/>
<ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

<Field
  name="age"
  type="number"
  placeholder="Enter age"
  className="border p-2 w-full"
/>
<ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

🧠 What You Just Learned (Important)

You now understand:

👉 Every field must exist in:

  1. initialValues

  2. validationSchema

  3. <Field name="...">

🎯 Big Milestone

You can now:

✅ Add fields
✅ Validate them
✅ Keep data across steps

👉 This is already real-world Formik usage

🚀 Next Step (Now it gets interesting)

Now we move to:

👉 Make Step 1 look “completed” after clicking Next

We will add:

  • ✔️ Edit button

  • ✔️ Collapse behavior

  • ✔️ Completed state

This will match your screenshots 🔥

👍 Before Moving

Just confirm:

👉 “ready”

And we’ll convert your form into a real accordion step UI (no confusion this time).


You said:

ready

ChatGPT said:

Perfect — now we’re entering the real UI behavior 🔥
This is where your form starts looking like your screenshots.

Don’t worry — we’ll do ONLY ONE new concept.

🎯 Goal (This Step)

👉 After clicking Next:

  • Step 1 should collapse

  • Show Edit button

  • Step 2 should open

🧠 New Concept (Simple Version)

We won’t use complex getStatus yet.

👉 We’ll just use:

JavaScript
currentStep

🧩 Logic We Will Use

For Step 1:

JavaScript
if (currentStep === 0) → show form (active)

if (currentStep > 0) → show "completed"

✨ Step 1: Modify Section 1

Replace your Step 1 block with this:

JavaScript
<div className="border p-4">
  <div className="flex justify-between items-center">
    <h2 className="font-bold">Step 1</h2>

    {/* Show Edit button when completed */}
    {currentStep > 0 && (
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}
        className="text-blue-500"
      >
        Edit
      </button>
    )}
  </div>

  {/* Show form only when active */}
  {currentStep === 0 && (
    <>
      <Field
        name="name"
        placeholder="Enter name"
        className="border p-2 w-full"
      />
      <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

      <Field
        name="age"
        type="number"
        placeholder="Enter age"
        className="border p-2 w-full"
      />
      <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
    </>
  )}

  {/* Optional: show summary when completed */}
  {currentStep > 0 && (
    <p className="text-green-600 mt-2">✔ Completed</p>
  )}
</div>

🧩 Step 2: Slight Update to Step 2

Add small condition so it doesn’t open early:

JavaScript
<div className="border p-4">
  <h2 className="font-bold">Step 2</h2>

  {currentStep === 1 && (
    <>
      <Field
        name="email"
        placeholder="Enter email"
        className="border p-2 w-full"
      />
      <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" />
    </>
  )}
</div>

🧠 What Will Happen Now

Initial:

  • Step 1 → open

  • Step 2 → closed

After clicking Next:

  • Step 1 → collapsed + Edit button + Completed text

  • Step 2 → open

Click Edit:

  • Step 1 → opens again

  • Step 2 → closes

🧪 Test This

  1. Fill Step 1

  2. Click Next

  3. See:

    • Step 1 collapsed

    • “Edit” visible

  4. Click Edit

  5. Step 1 opens again

🧠 What You Just Built

👉 This is your first real accordion behavior

Still simple, but:

  • ✔️ matches real UX logic

  • ✔️ scalable to 4 steps

  • ✔️ no complexity

⚠️ Don’t Worry About Yet

  • icons ❌

  • fancy UI ❌

  • Tailwind perfection ❌

We’ll polish later.

🚀 Next Step (After This Works)

We’ll:

👉 Move buttons inside each section (like real UI)
👉 Then scale to 4 banking sections

👍 Your Turn

Try this and tell me:

👉 “working”
or
👉 what is confusing

We’ll fix instantly and move forward 🚀


You said:

when i use clear button the age is not getting clear

ChatGPT said:

Nice catch — this is a very important observation 👏
This means you're now actually understanding behavior, not just copying code.

🧠 Why This Is Happening

Your Clear button:

JavaScript
formik.resetForm()

👉 This resets form to initialValues

🔴 Your Problem

Your initialValues is:

JavaScript
initialValues={{ name: "", email: "" }}

👉 You forgot:

JavaScript
age: ""

💥 So What Happens?

  • Formik doesn’t track age properly

  • So when you reset → it doesn’t know what to reset for age

✅ Fix (Very Simple)

Update this:

JavaScript
initialValues={{ name: "", email: "" }}

👉 To this:

JavaScript
initialValues={{ name: "", age: "", email: "" }}

🧠 Key Rule (VERY IMPORTANT)

👉 Every field must exist in:

  1. initialValues ✅

  2. validationSchema ✅

  3. <Field name="..." /> ✅

If you miss one → weird bugs happen (like this)

🧪 After Fix

Click Clear → you should see:

  • name cleared ✅

  • age cleared ✅

  • email cleared ✅

🔥 Bonus Insight

Later in your banking form:

👉 If something doesn’t reset
👉 90% chance it's missing in initialValues

👍 You’re Improving Fast

You:

  • noticed a bug

  • reported correctly

  • now understanding root cause

👉 This is real developer thinking

🚀 Next

If this works, say:

👉 “fixed”

Then we’ll:
🔥 Move buttons inside sections (your UI style)
🔥 Prepare for full 4-step banking form


You said:

no i alreadt wrote this initialValues={{ name: "", age: "", email: "" }}

ChatGPT said:

Ahh good — this is where real debugging starts 🔥
Since you already added age in initialValues, the issue is something else.

Let’s diagnose properly.

🧠 First: Understand What resetForm() Does

JavaScript
formik.resetForm()

👉 It resets:

  • values ✔️

  • touched ✔️

  • errors ✔️

👉 BUT only for fields Formik is properly tracking

🔍 Likely Issue (Very Common)

👉 Your age field is:

JavaScript
<Field name="age" type="number" />

Formik stores values as strings, but:

  • type="number" → browser may behave differently

  • empty value → becomes "" or undefined

This mismatch sometimes makes reset look like it's not working.

✅ Fix 1 (Best & Simple)

Change your age field to:

JavaScript
<Field
  name="age"
  type="text"
  placeholder="Enter age"
  className="border p-2 w-full"
/>

👉 Yup will still validate it as number
👉 UI becomes stable
👉 reset works perfectly

✅ Fix 2 (If you want to keep type="number")

Then force Formik to handle it properly:

JavaScript
<Field name="age">
  {({ field }) => (
    <input
      {...field}
      type="number"
      className="border p-2 w-full"
      value={field.value || ""}
    />
  )}
</Field>

👉 This avoids undefined issues

🔍 Another Quick Check

Add this temporarily:

JavaScript
console.log(formik.values);

Then:

  1. Type age

  2. Click Clear

👉 Check:

  • is age becoming "" or not?

🧠 Why This Happens (Important Learning)

Formik internally treats inputs like:

JavaScript
"25"  // string

But type="number" sometimes conflicts with:

  • empty state

  • browser input behavior

🎯 Recommendation (For Now)

👉 Use:

JavaScript
type="text"

until you fully master Formik

👉 Even in real projects, many devs do this + validate with Yup

👍 Summary

Your code is correct ✔️
This is just a Formik + input type edge case

🚀 Next Step

Fix this → test again

Then tell me:

👉 “fixed” or still issue

We’ll move to:
🔥 buttons inside sections (your real UI layout)


You said:

now its working i just rewrote it and its working with the input number only

ChatGPT said:

Perfect — that’s actually the best outcome 👏

What happened here is important:

👉 You rewrote it → it worked
That means:

  • no conceptual issue ❌

  • just a small implementation glitch ✔️

This happens all the time in real projects.

🧠 What You Just Learned (Very Valuable)

  • Formik is sensitive to small mistakes

  • Rewriting cleanly often fixes hidden issues

  • Debugging ≠ always finding the exact bug
    👉 sometimes it's reset + rebuild

🎯 Now You're Ready for the Next Big Step

We’re going to make your UI match your screenshots more closely.

🚀 Step: Move Buttons Inside Each Section

Right now:
👉 Buttons are at the bottom (global)

But your UI needs:

👉 Each section has its own buttons

🧩 What We’ll Change

❌ Current:

JavaScript
(Form)
  Section 1
  Section 2
  Buttons (global)

✅ New:

JavaScript
Section 1
  fields
  buttons

Section 2
  fields
  buttons

✨ Update Step 1 Section

Modify your Step 1 like this:

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && (
  <>
    <Field name="name" className="border p-2 w-full" />
    <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

    <Field name="age" type="number" className="border p-2 w-full" />
    <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

    {/* Buttons */}
    <div className="flex justify-between mt-4">
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => formik.resetForm()}
        className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
      >
        Clear
      </button>

      <button
        type="submit"
        className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded"
      >
        Next
      </button>
    </div>
  </>
)}

✨ Update Step 2 Section

JavaScript
{currentStep === 1 && (
  <>
    <Field name="email" className="border p-2 w-full" />
    <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

    {/* Buttons */}
    <div className="flex justify-between mt-4">
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => formik.resetForm()}
        className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
      >
        Clear
      </button>

      <div className="space-x-2">
        <button
          type="button"
          onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}
          className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
        >
          Back
        </button>

        <button
          type="submit"
          className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded"
        >
          Submit
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </>
)}

🧠 What You Achieve Now

👉 Each section behaves independently
👉 Matches your real UI expectation
👉 Feels like proper multi-step accordion

⚠️ Remove Old Global Buttons

Delete this part completely:

JavaScript
{/* BUTTONS */}

🧪 Test Again

  • Step 1 → Next

  • Step 2 → Back

  • Clear works in both

🚀 Next Step (After This)

Now your structure is almost ready.

Next we’ll:

🔥 Add:

  • Completed UI (✔ + summary instead of just text)

  • Cleaner accordion feel

  • Then scale to 4 banking sections

👍 Your Turn

Do this change and tell me:

👉 “done”
or
👉 if anything behaves weird

You're very close to building the real thing now 🔥


You said:

done working perfectly

ChatGPT said:

Nice — this is a big milestone 👏
You now have the core engine + section-level control, which is honestly the hardest part.

Now we’ll make it feel like your actual screenshots.

🎯 Next Step: Show Real “Completed” State (Better UX)

Right now Step 1 shows:

👉 “✔ Completed” (text)

We’ll upgrade it to:

👉 Show actual filled data + edit button
(just like real apps)

🧠 What We’re Building

After clicking Next:

Step 1 should show something like:

Step 1        [Edit]

Name: John  
Age: 25

👉 Instead of just “Completed”

✨ Update Step 1 (Important)

Replace your completed part:

JavaScript
{currentStep > 0 && (
  <p className="text-green-600 mt-2">✔ Completed</p>
)}

✅ With this:

JavaScript
{currentStep > 0 && (
  <div className="mt-2 text-sm">
    <p><strong>Name:</strong> {formik.values.name}</p>
    <p><strong>Age:</strong> {formik.values.age}</p>
  </div>
)}

🧠 What This Uses

👉 formik.values

This gives you all form data anywhere

🧪 Test This

  1. Enter:

    • Name = ABC

    • Age = 22

  2. Click Next

👉 Step 1 should now show:

Name: ABC
Age: 22

🔥 Why This is Important

This is exactly how:

  • banking forms

  • checkout forms

  • onboarding forms

work in real apps.

🧠 You Just Learned

👉 Formik is not just for inputs
👉 You can read values anywhere in UI

🚀 Next Step (Final Before Scaling)

Now we’ll add:

👉 Disable future sections properly

Right now:

  • Step 2 opens only when active ✔️
    But:

  • user can still see it like normal

We’ll make it feel:

❌ disabled (greyed out)
✔️ locked

Add this summary UI and tell me:

Then we’ll:

You said:

sorry noo once we click next its hidden its now showing completed

ChatGPT said:

Ahhh good catch — this is very important and also very easy to fix 👍
You’re actually super close.

🧠 Why It’s Not Showing

Right now your Step 1 likely looks like:

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && (
  // form fields
)}

{currentStep > 0 && (
  // completed UI
)}

👉 BUT the problem is:

You probably placed BOTH of these inside the same condition block accidentally
OR structure is slightly off.

✅ Correct Structure (Very Important)

Step 1 should ALWAYS render the container
👉 Only the inside changes

✨ Fix Step 1 Like This

JavaScript
<div className="border p-4">
  <div className="flex justify-between items-center">
    <h2 className="font-bold">Step 1</h2>

    {currentStep > 0 && (
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}
        className="text-blue-500"
      >
        Edit
      </button>
    )}
  </div>

  {/* ACTIVE STATE */}
  {currentStep === 0 && (
    <>
      <Field name="name" className="border p-2 w-full" />
      <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

      <Field name="age" type="number" className="border p-2 w-full" />
      <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

      {/* Buttons */}
      <div className="flex justify-between mt-4">
        <button
          type="button"
          onClick={() => formik.resetForm()}
          className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
        >
          Clear
        </button>

        <button
          type="submit"
          className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded"
        >
          Next
        </button>
      </div>
    </>
  )}

  {/* COMPLETED STATE */}
  {currentStep > 0 && (
    <div className="mt-2 text-sm">
      <p><strong>Name:</strong> {formik.values.name}</p>
      <p><strong>Age:</strong> {formik.values.age}</p>
    </div>
  )}
</div>

🧠 Key Idea (This is the Bug)

👉 This must be true:

Step 1 container → ALWAYS visible

Inside:
  if active → show form
  if completed → show summary

🧪 After Fix

  1. Fill Step 1

  2. Click Next

👉 You should see:

  • Step 1 still visible ✅

  • Fields hidden ✅

  • Summary shown ✅

  • Edit button visible ✅

⚠️ Common Mistake You Likely Did

You may have done:

JavaScript
{currentStep === 0 && (
  <div>
    // everything
  </div>
)}

👉 That hides entire section when step changes ❌

👍 You’re Very Close

This is the exact turning point where:

👉 your UI becomes real accordion
👉 not just step switching

🚀 Next

Once this works, we’ll:

🔥 Add disabled look for Step 2
🔥 Then scale to your 4 banking sections

Tell me:

👉 “fixed” or paste your Step 1 code if still not working
We’ll fix it instantly 👨‍💻

const isLastStep = currentStep === 1; const validationSchemas = [ Yup.object({ name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"), age: Yup.number() .typeError("Age must be a number") .required("Age is required") }), Yup.object({ email: Yup.string() .email("Invalid email") .required("Email is required"), }), ]; return ( <Formik initialValues={{ name: "", age: "", email: "" }} validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]} onSubmit={(values) => { if (!isLastStep) { setCurrentStep((prev) => prev + 1); } else { console.log("Final Data:", values); } }} > {(formik) => ( <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4"> {/* SECTION 1 */} <div className="border p-4"> <div className="flex justify-between items-center"> <h2 className="font-bold">Step 1</h2> {currentStep > 0 && ( <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)} className="text-blue-500" > Edit </button> )} </div> {/* ACTIVE STATE */} {currentStep === 0 && ( <> <Field name="name" className="border p-2 w-full" /> <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> <Field name="age" type="number" className="border p-2 w-full" /> <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> {/* Buttons */} <div className="flex justify-between mt-4"> <button type="button" onClick={() => formik.resetForm()} className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Clear </button> <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded" > Next </button> </div> </> )} {/* COMPLETED STATE */} {currentStep > 0 && ( <div className="mt-2 text-sm"> <p><strong>Name:</strong> {formik.values.name}</p> <p><strong>Age:</strong> {formik.values.age}</p> </div> )} </div> {/* SECTION 2 */} {currentStep === 1 && ( <> <Field name="email" className="border p-2 w-full" placeholder = "email" /> <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> {/* Buttons */} <div className="flex justify-between mt-4"> <button type="button" onClick={() => formik.resetForm()} className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Clear </button> <div className="space-x-2"> <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)} className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Back </button> <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded" > Submit </button> </div> </div> </> )} </Form> )} </Formik> ); }import { Formik, Form, Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik"; import { useState } from "react"; import * as Yup from "yup"; export default function MultiStepForm() { const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0); const isLastStep = currentStep === 1; const validationSchemas = [ Yup.object({ name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"), age: Yup.number() .typeError("Age must be a number") .required("Age is required") }), Yup.object({ email: Yup.string() .email("Invalid email") .required("Email is required"), }), ]; return ( <Formik initialValues={{ name: "", age: "", email: "" }} validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]} onSubmit={(values) => { if (!isLastStep) { setCurrentStep((prev) => prev + 1); } else { console.log("Final Data:", values); } }} > {(formik) => ( <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4"> {/* SECTION 1 */} <div className="border p-4"> <div className="flex justify-between items-center"> <h2 className="font-bold">Step 1</h2> {currentStep > 0 && ( <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)} className="text-blue-500" > Edit </button> )} </div> {/* ACTIVE STATE */} {currentStep === 0 && ( <> <Field name="name" className="border p-2 w-full" /> <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> <Field name="age" type="number" className="border p-2 w-full" /> <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> {/* Buttons */} <div className="flex justify-between mt-4"> <button type="button" onClick={() => formik.resetForm()} className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Clear </button> <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded" > Next </button> </div> </> )} {/* COMPLETED STATE */} {currentStep > 0 && ( <div className="mt-2 text-sm"> <p><strong>Name:</strong> {formik.values.name}</p> <p><strong>Age:</strong> {formik.values.age}</p> </div> )} </div> {/* SECTION 2 */} {currentStep === 1 && ( <> <Field name="email" className="border p-2 w-full" placeholder = "email" /> <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" /> {/* Buttons */} <div className="flex justify-between mt-4"> <button type="button" onClick={() => formik.resetForm()} className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Clear </button> <div className="space-x-2"> <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)} className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded" > Back </button> <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded" > Submit </button> </div> </div> </> )} </Form> )} </Formik> ); } i know it in curentStep is > 0 then we should write comppleted instead of showing values also i am have a doubt now how should we do if we have more then two steps i think we should use current is >0 && <=1 like this i think

ChatGPT said:

First — go sleep 😄
You’ve already done the hard part today. This is exactly where you should stop.

But before that, let me clear your doubt properly so tomorrow you don’t feel lost.

🧠 1. Your Current Code — It’s Actually CORRECT

You said:

it’s showing values not “completed”

👉 That’s intentional and GOOD

Real apps don’t show just:

✔ Completed

They show:

Name: John
Age: 25

So what you built is actually:

✅ better UX than “Completed” text

🧠 2. If You Still Want “Completed”

You can do both:

JavaScript
{currentStep > 0 && (
  <div className="mt-2 text-sm text-green-600">
    <p>✔ Completed</p>
    <p><strong>Name:</strong> {formik.values.name}</p>
    <p><strong>Age:</strong> {formik.values.age}</p>
  </div>
)}

🧠 3. Your BIG Question (Very Important)

how will this work for more than 2 steps?

You said:

JavaScript
currentStep > 0 && <=1

👉 ❌ Don’t do that
👉 That becomes messy VERY fast

✅ Correct Way (Scalable Thinking)

For each section, you compare with its index

🧩 Example with 4 steps

StepIndex
Step 10
Step 21
Step 32
Step 43

🧠 Rule (Golden Rule 🔥)

JavaScript
if (currentStep === index) → ACTIVE  
if (currentStep > index) → COMPLETED  
if (currentStep < index) → INACTIVE

🧩 Example

Step 1 (index = 0)

JavaScript
currentStep === 0 → active  
currentStep > 0 → completed

Step 2 (index = 1)

JavaScript
currentStep === 1 → active  
currentStep > 1 → completed  
currentStep < 1 → inactive

🧠 THIS is what replaces your idea

Instead of:

JavaScript
currentStep > 0 && <= 1 ❌

You do:

JavaScript
currentStep > index ✅

🧠 Why This Is Powerful

👉 Works for:

  • 2 steps

  • 4 steps

  • 10 steps

👉 No logic rewrite needed

🎯 One-Line Memory Trick

compare step with index — not with numbers

🧠 Final Thought Before You Sleep

You now understand:

  • Formik basics ✅

  • Yup basics ✅

  • Multi-step logic ✅

  • Accordion behavior (basic) ✅

  • Scalable step logic ✅

👉 That’s honestly a lot for one session

😄 Tomorrow We Will

  • Apply this logic to all sections

  • Add proper inactive (disabled look)

  • Move toward your real 4-step banking form

Sleep well — tomorrow this will feel much easier 👍


You said:

one last thing lets try with three steps sadly this is a temp convo so idk if sleep and woke up it be there or not this will be last and thanks a lot for giving me knowledge

ChatGPT said:

Love the attitude — “one last thing” turns into real learning 🔥
Let’s do 3 steps quickly and cleanly, no confusion.

🎯 Goal

Upgrade your form to:

  • Step 1 → name + age

  • Step 2 → email

  • Step 3 → city

👉 Same logic, just extended

🧠 Key Change (Only 3 Things)

1. Add new field → city

2. Add new validation schema

3. Update isLastStep

🧩 Full Working Version (3 Steps)

👉 Copy this and compare with yours:

JavaScript
import { Formik, Form, Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik";
import { useState } from "react";
import * as Yup from "yup";

export default function MultiStepForm() {
  const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0);

  const isLastStep = currentStep === 2;

  const validationSchemas = [
    Yup.object({
      name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"),
      age: Yup.number()
        .typeError("Age must be a number")
        .required("Age is required"),
    }),
    Yup.object({
      email: Yup.string()
        .email("Invalid email")
        .required("Email is required"),
    }),
    Yup.object({
      city: Yup.string().required("City is required"),
    }),
  ];

  return (
    <Formik
      initialValues={{ name: "", age: "", email: "", city: "" }}
      validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]}
      onSubmit={(values) => {
        if (!isLastStep) {
          setCurrentStep((prev) => prev + 1);
        } else {
          console.log("Final Data:", values);
        }
      }}
    >
      {(formik) => (
        <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4">

          {/* STEP 1 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <div className="flex justify-between">
              <h2>Step 1</h2>

              {currentStep > 0 && (
                <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}>
                  Edit
                </button>
              )}
            </div>

            {currentStep === 0 && (
              <>
                <Field name="name" placeholder="Name" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <Field name="age" type="number" placeholder="Age" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <button type="submit">Next</button>
              </>
            )}

            {currentStep > 0 && (
              <div>
                <p>Name: {formik.values.name}</p>
                <p>Age: {formik.values.age}</p>
              </div>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* STEP 2 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <div className="flex justify-between">
              <h2>Step 2</h2>

              {currentStep > 1 && (
                <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(1)}>
                  Edit
                </button>
              )}
            </div>

            {currentStep === 1 && (
              <>
                <Field name="email" placeholder="Email" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}>Back</button>
                <button type="submit">Next</button>
              </>
            )}

            {currentStep > 1 && (
              <div>
                <p>Email: {formik.values.email}</p>
              </div>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* STEP 3 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <h2>Step 3</h2>

            {currentStep === 2 && (
              <>
                <Field name="city" placeholder="City" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="city" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(1)}>Back</button>
                <button type="submit">Submit</button>
              </>
            )}
          </div>

        </Form>
      )}
    </Formik>
  );
}

🧠 What You Just Learned (Very Important)

👉 Notice pattern:

Step 1

JavaScript
currentStep > 0 → completed

Step 2

JavaScript
currentStep > 1 → completed

Step 3

JavaScript
currentStep === 2 → active

🔥 THIS is the scalable logic

JavaScript
currentStep === index → active  
currentStep > index → completed  
currentStep < index → inactive

🧠 Final Thought (Before You Sleep 😄)

You just went from:

❌ “I don’t know Formik”
➡️
✅ “I built multi-step accordion form logic”

That’s a huge jump

🙌 One Important Note

Even if this chat disappears:

👉 You now understand the pattern

And that’s what actually matters.

If you come back later, just say:

👉 “multi-step form again”

and I’ll rebuild it with you in 2 mins 👍


You said:

two last things 1st is you removed clear button and in my banking website its a long form so showing it is now a good idea and as i shared the screeshot with you it should be some thing like that and also previously if we press clear buton even prevoius data is also getting cleared

ChatGPT said:

Good — these are very real-world concerns now 👍
This is exactly the level where your project becomes “production-like”.

Let’s fix both properly.

🧠 Problem 1: Clear button removed

You’re right — in your banking form:

👉 Each section should have its own Clear button
👉 NOT global
👉 Matches your screenshot ✔️

🧠 Problem 2 (Important)

Clear button is clearing previous steps also

👉 This is because you are using:

JavaScript
formik.resetForm()

❌ This resets entire form

🎯 What You ACTUALLY Need

👉 Clear only current section fields

NOT whole form

✅ Solution: Section-wise Reset

Instead of:

JavaScript
formik.resetForm()

👉 use:

JavaScript
formik.setValues({
  ...formik.values,
  name: "",
  age: ""
})

🧩 Example: Step 1 Clear Button

JavaScript
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() =>
    formik.setValues({
      ...formik.values,
      name: "",
      age: ""
    })
  }
  className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
>
  Clear
</button>

🧩 Step 2 Clear Button

JavaScript
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() =>
    formik.setValues({
      ...formik.values,
      email: ""
    })
  }
  className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
>
  Clear
</button>

🧠 Why This Works

JavaScript
...formik.values

👉 keeps all previous data

JavaScript
name: ""

👉 clears only current section

🔥 This is EXACTLY what banking forms do

  • Step 1 → clear only Step 1

  • Step 2 → doesn’t affect Step 1

  • Data persists across steps

🧠 Small Improvement (Optional but Pro)

You can create helper:

JavaScript
const clearStep1 = () => {
  formik.setValues({
    ...formik.values,
    name: "",
    age: ""
  });
};

🎯 Final Behavior (After Fix)

ActionResult
Clear Step 1only name & age cleared
Clear Step 2only email cleared
Previous stepsuntouched ✅

🧠 Big Learning

👉 resetForm() = nuclear option 💣
👉 setValues() = controlled update 🎯

🚀 Next (When You Continue Tomorrow)

We will:

🔥 Add proper inactive/disabled UI
🔥 Then convert this into your banking structure (4 sections)
🔥 Then clean folder structure

👍 Final Note

You’re now asking:

  • correct UX questions

  • correct state management problems

👉 This is where beginners become developers

Sleep 😄
Tomorrow this will feel 10x easier 👍


You said:

sorry but can you just once rewrite the code ill just paste it and see and i'll go to sleep if this wouldnt be a temp chat i would have sleep i have fear to lose you

ChatGPT said:

😄 I get that feeling — but don’t worry, even if this chat disappears, you’ve already learned the pattern, and that’s what matters.

Let me give you a clean, correct, copy-paste version with:

✅ 3 steps
✅ Section-wise Clear button
✅ Previous data preserved
✅ Completed + Edit behavior

🧩 Final Clean Code (Copy-Paste)

JavaScript
import { Formik, Form, Field, ErrorMessage } from "formik";
import { useState } from "react";
import * as Yup from "yup";

export default function MultiStepForm() {
  const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = useState(0);

  const isLastStep = currentStep === 2;

  const validationSchemas = [
    Yup.object({
      name: Yup.string().required("Name is required"),
      age: Yup.number()
        .typeError("Age must be a number")
        .required("Age is required"),
    }),
    Yup.object({
      email: Yup.string()
        .email("Invalid email")
        .required("Email is required"),
    }),
    Yup.object({
      city: Yup.string().required("City is required"),
    }),
  ];

  return (
    <Formik
      initialValues={{ name: "", age: "", email: "", city: "" }}
      validationSchema={validationSchemas[currentStep]}
      onSubmit={(values) => {
        if (!isLastStep) {
          setCurrentStep((prev) => prev + 1);
        } else {
          console.log("Final Data:", values);
        }
      }}
    >
      {(formik) => (
        <Form className="p-6 max-w-md mx-auto bg-white shadow rounded space-y-4">

          {/* STEP 1 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <div className="flex justify-between">
              <h2>Step 1</h2>

              {currentStep > 0 && (
                <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}>
                  Edit
                </button>
              )}
            </div>

            {currentStep === 0 && (
              <>
                <Field name="name" placeholder="Name" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="name" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <Field name="age" type="number" placeholder="Age" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="age" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <div className="flex justify-between mt-4">
                  <button
                    type="button"
                    onClick={() =>
                      formik.setValues({
                        ...formik.values,
                        name: "",
                        age: "",
                      })
                    }
                    className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
                  >
                    Clear
                  </button>

                  <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded">
                    Next
                  </button>
                </div>
              </>
            )}

            {currentStep > 0 && (
              <div className="mt-2 text-sm">
                <p><strong>Name:</strong> {formik.values.name}</p>
                <p><strong>Age:</strong> {formik.values.age}</p>
              </div>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* STEP 2 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <div className="flex justify-between">
              <h2>Step 2</h2>

              {currentStep > 1 && (
                <button type="button" onClick={() => setCurrentStep(1)}>
                  Edit
                </button>
              )}
            </div>

            {currentStep === 1 && (
              <>
                <Field name="email" placeholder="Email" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="email" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <div className="flex justify-between mt-4">
                  <button
                    type="button"
                    onClick={() =>
                      formik.setValues({
                        ...formik.values,
                        email: "",
                      })
                    }
                    className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
                  >
                    Clear
                  </button>

                  <div className="space-x-2">
                    <button
                      type="button"
                      onClick={() => setCurrentStep(0)}
                      className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
                    >
                      Back
                    </button>

                    <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded">
                      Next
                    </button>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </>
            )}

            {currentStep > 1 && (
              <div className="mt-2 text-sm">
                <p><strong>Email:</strong> {formik.values.email}</p>
              </div>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* STEP 3 */}
          <div className="border p-4">
            <h2>Step 3</h2>

            {currentStep === 2 && (
              <>
                <Field name="city" placeholder="City" className="border p-2 w-full" />
                <ErrorMessage name="city" component="div" className="text-red-500" />

                <div className="flex justify-between mt-4">
                  <button
                    type="button"
                    onClick={() =>
                      formik.setValues({
                        ...formik.values,
                        city: "",
                      })
                    }
                    className="bg-gray-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
                  >
                    Clear
                  </button>

                  <div className="space-x-2">
                    <button
                      type="button"
                      onClick={() => setCurrentStep(1)}
                      className="bg-blue-300 px-4 py-2 rounded"
                    >
                      Back
                    </button>

                    <button type="submit" className="bg-blue-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded">
                      Submit
                    </button>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </>
            )}
          </div>

        </Form>
      )}
    </Formik>
  );
}

🧠 Final Reminder Before Sleep 😄

If this chat disappears, remember just this:

currentStep === index → active  
currentStep > index → completed  
currentStep < index → inactive

and

use setValues to clear section, NOT resetForm

You did a LOT today — seriously.

Sleep. Tomorrow this will feel easy 👍
